headline: USA RARE EARTH SEIZES POLE POSITION: $2.8B Serra Verde Grab Locks Up Only Non-Asia Source of All 4 Magnetic REEs
snippet: USA Rare Earth (NASDAQ: USAR) agreed to acquire Brazil's Serra Verde Group for ~$2.8B ($300M cash + shares), giving it the only non-Asian producer capable of supplying all four magnetic rare earths — neodymium, praseodymium, dysprosium, and terbium — at scale. The deal, announced April 20, includes a 15-year U.S. government-backed offtake for 100% of output; shares surged 11% April 22 after Canaccord reiterated its Buy rating.

headline: GOLD RECLAIMS $4,750 After Iran Truce Extension — Second Round of Talks Collapses, Metal Eyes $5,000
snippet: Spot gold recovered to $4,759/oz on April 22 after President Trump extended the two-week U.S.-Iran ceasefire, though plans for a second negotiating round collapsed immediately, leaving the geopolitical premium intact. The metal had slid toward $4,700 the prior session on a firmer dollar and uncertainty around the Senate confirmation hearing of incoming Fed Chair Kevin Warsh, who flagged a new anti-inflation framework.

headline: F3 URANIUM EXTENDS TETRA ZONE: Mineralization Hit in 3 of 6 Step-Outs at Patterson Lake North
snippet: F3 Uranium (TSX-V: FUU) intersected uranium mineralization in three of six step-out holes testing along-strike and down-plunge extensions of the Tetra Zone at its 100%-owned Patterson Lake North project, Athabasca Basin, Saskatchewan. Hole PLN26-226 returned 0.5m of radioactivity up to 1,400 cps at 470m depth, 275m along strike from a 2025 intercept that returned 13.0m of 0.28% U3O8 including 3.0m of 1.19% U3O8.

headline: GALWAY METALS DRILLS 6.1 g/t Gold Over 19m at Clarence Stream — Near-Surface Zone Widens in New Brunswick
snippet: Galway Metals (TSX-V: GWM) reported 6.1 g/t Au over 19.0m including 18.3 g/t over 3.0m from the South Deposit at its Clarence Stream gold project in New Brunswick, stepping out ~30m from hole CS-416 which previously returned 28.0m at 6.1 g/t Au. Both holes sit within 50 vertical metres of surface and inside the 2022 resource pit shell, suggesting the project's 2.26 Moz resource estimate has room to grow.

headline: RAMP METALS DRILLS 21m of 0.80% Copper, 17 g/t Silver, 0.73% Zinc in Saskatchewan VMS Debut
snippet: Ramp Metals (CSE: RAMP) drilled 0.80% Cu, 17.01 g/t Ag, 0.09 g/t Au, and 0.73% Zn over 21m from 50.5–71.5m depth in hole Rush-011, the first hole of its winter program at Rottenstone SW in Saskatchewan. A 3m interval graded 1.50% Cu, 24.15 g/t Ag, confirming VMS-style mineralization along the Rush trend with a newly identified deeper zone to the west.

headline: DIPS BOUGHT: Gold Finds Support Above $4,700 as Buyers Step In — GoldSeek Reads the Tape
snippet: GoldSeek's market commentary flags a swift recovery in gold from the mid-$4,600s to above $4,750 as discretionary buyers treated the Iran-driven selloff as a buying opportunity rather than a trend change. The piece notes that silver outperformed on the bounce — up nearly 2% vs gold's 1% — signalling risk appetite returning to the metals complex.

headline: SIX YEARS AND COUNTING: Silver Poised for Record 6th Consecutive Annual Supply Deficit in 2026
snippet: The Silver Institute projects silver's structural deficit will reach 46–67 million ounces in 2026, marking a sixth straight year of supply falling short of demand as solar, EV, and AI data-centre fabrication absorb more metal than mines and recycling can produce. Since 2021, over 762 million troy ounces have been drawn from global above-ground stocks to cover the gap — a drawdown that analysts say is unsustainable at current prices.

headline: PEAK GOLD or New Supply Cycle? The Answer Will Define Junior Miners for the Next Decade
snippet: A CEOWORLD analysis challenges the popular 'peak gold' thesis, examining whether the plateau in global mine production since 2019 reflects a permanent supply ceiling or simply the lag between exploration dollars and new output — with the current junior exploration boom potentially seeding a new cycle by 2028–30. The piece is useful context for retail investors weighing whether rising gold prices will eventually attract enough capital to cap the bull market.

headline: CHINA KEEPS STACKING: Gold Imports Rise for Third Straight Month as Central Bank Buying Continues
snippet: Chinese gold imports climbed for a third consecutive month in early 2026, with the People's Bank of China continuing to add to reserves at a pace analysts estimate at 50–60 tonnes per month — consistent with the 790+ tonnes imported in Q1. Money Metals notes that Chinese central bank demand alone is consuming a meaningful share of annual mine supply, providing a structural floor under prices largely independent of Western investor sentiment.

headline: WELLS FARGO GOES BOLD: 'Debasement Trade' Could Drive Gold to $8,000, Bank's Strategists Say
snippet: Wells Fargo strategists published a bull-case scenario for gold reaching $8,000/oz, nearly double current levels near $4,800, driven by currency debasement, ongoing central bank de-dollarisation, and sustained fiscal deficits in the U.S. and Europe. The bank's base case for 2026 sits in the $6,000–$6,300 range; the $8,000 figure assumes an accelerated breakdown in confidence in fiat currencies rather than a simple demand uptick.

headline: EARTH DAY WARNING: Billions of Lithium Batteries Are Going to Landfill — Recycling May Trump New Mining
snippet: An Earth Day analysis argues that the rush to mine new lithium is being undermined by a systemic failure to recover lithium from end-of-life batteries, with millions of lithium-ion cells entering landfills annually despite containing material that is both economically valuable and increasingly scarce. The piece examines whether tightening battery-recycling regulations — already advancing in the EU and several U.S. states — could reshape the supply picture for junior lithium explorers whose projects are priced assuming perpetual primary-mining demand.
